_meta.purpose: Briefing-quality synthesis of the ERP program. Answers any meeting question in 30 seconds per pillar. Sits above erp_pillar_baselines.json (which holds operational detail) and feeds both live UI pages and PPTX generation.
_meta.pillarStructureNote: As of 2026-03-04, restructured from 4 pillars (P2P, Reporting, Controls, Governance) to 3 functional workstream pillars (P2P, Record to Report, Reporting & Planning). Controls/SOX is now a compliance design layer within each pillar — not a top-level pillar. Program governance/mobilization status lives in programState.topProgramRisks and the header strip. This aligns with the board-level story: we are replacing three broken manual processes in Sage by Jan 1 2027, with SOX-compliant design baked in from day one.
_meta.refreshInstructions: When new context arrives (emails, meeting notes, vendor decks, SharePoint artifacts, CFTI updates), a Claude session should: (1) read this file + erp_pillar_baselines.json + the new context, (2) update the relevant pillar's currentStateSummary if material facts changed, (3) move items from currentStateUnknown to currentStateKnown if evidence arrived, (4) update keyDecisionsPending and nextMoves, (5) update lastRefreshedAt and synthesizedAt, (6) add a note to changeLog. Do not rewrite everything — only update what actually changed.

Priority: sign NetSuite V3 SOW by March 31, consolidate CFGI dual proposals into one engagement, confirm Phase 1/2 scope boundary, obtain budget authorization from Didier and Hema.

keyPeople.financeLead: Hema Keshava (Finance Lead — FP&A, Treasury, Tax, Accounting)

pillars.label: P2P / Transaction Operations
pillars.currentStateSummary: France SA processes ~4,000 invoices/year and 650 POs across disconnected systems (Sage 100, DocuShare, Trustpair, Concur, Agicap) with no system-to-system flow. In practice, the working control layer is DocuShare approval emails plus Juliette's master Excel tracker, where approved POs are logged and invoices are manually checked before booking in Sage. Current authorization is amount-based only, not tied to department or cost center. If an invoice exceeds the PO, Juliette chases explanation manually and allows some leeway. Only Juliette and Fatma can create vendors in Sage; Juliette also enters bank information there, with Trustpair checking banking data twice per day. Reporting is effectively absent across France P2P, including formal aging.
pillars.futureStateSummary: Phase 1 ERP (SOX-first, per Trinidad): NetSuite should replace the DocuShare plus spreadsheet control layer with native France PO management, approval routing by threshold and department or cost center, invoice-to-PO tolerance rules, vendor workflow, Trustpair-compatible validation, and usable P2P reporting. DoA design remains a pre-ERP governance deliverable, but the ERP should enforce the resulting matrix. The France AP objective is not just control closure; it is replacing manual operational dependency on Juliette's workbook with system workflow and reporting.
pillars.whyItMatters: P2P is the highest-density area of ERP-addressable control gaps (5 of 9 ERP-signal controls). It is also where material weakness indicators exist on vendor master and approval workflow. Getting this right is the most direct path from 'manual + risky' to 'system-enforced + auditable'.

currentStateKnown.fact: Vendor master confirmed at 497 vendors (Trustpair export 'Base supplier Trustpair.xlsx', received 2026-03-02 from Philippe Goncalves). Geography: FR 245 (49%), US 99 (20%), GB 33 (7%), DE 27 (5%).

Trustpair validation results: 475/497 globally Favorable (95.6%). 22 Défavorable couple — bank account exists but ownership by supplier not confirmed. 10 Défavorable/Anomalie global (2 closed bank accounts, 4 invalid IBAN format, 4 missing company ID for non-FR entities). Zero vendors on blocklist.
FR-PTP-02G control evidence confirmed: Trustpair actively validates all 497 vendors on bank account integrity. Audit evidence is exportable from Trustpair platform.

Active contract commitments: €165,055,532 EUR across 2,888 active lines and 187 unique vendors (ENGAGE_2025 register, 31-Dec-2025). €7,490,467 already booked. Grand total EURO-equivalent across all currencies: €182.8M.

ENGAGE commitment breakdown by department: CLI (Clinical) 1,265 lines, CMC 1,014, LAB 334, DAT 65, QA 111, REG 34. Currencies: EUROS 79%, USD 15%, GBP 3%. 3,042 closed lines in 'Presta clôturées' sheet.
Trustpair NetSuite integration confirmed as in-scope (per KPMG deck, Feb 26 2026). (_archived)
Invoice volume: ~4,000/year (France SA). PO volume: 650 raised in 2025.
Total contract commitments: $200M+ — not tracked in any system; lives in contracts in DocuShare.
Active vendors: <500. Vendor master cleaned in 2025. Second cleanup scheduled March 2026 (Philippe will provide XLS report).
France SA payment cycle: two campaigns per month, 10th and 25th.
System landscape: Sage 100 (GL/invoicing), DocuShare (documents), Trustpair (vendor validation), Concur (expenses). Zero integration feeds between any of them.
Juliette and Fatma perform all manual handoffs via shared invoice inbox (monitored with Philippe).
Sage 100 cannot verify vendor banking information — structurally incapable.
Trustpair handles bank validation AND full vendor audit per wire transfer. Cost <$15K/year. Load-bearing control.
Approval workflows are not in Sage — entirely manual and paper/email-based.
No PO requirement for contractors (high volume, industry-typical in biotech/CRO). No POs for contractors = commitments off the books.
Some CRO contracts processed without POs — Trinidad views PO coverage for CRO as critical.
PO process flows through DocuShare with no systematic capture of amounts. Juliette handles manually.
Juliette maintains a master Excel sheet that tracks payments and acts as the real PO and invoice matching layer after DocuShare approval emails are received.
Current PO approval authorization is based on amount only. Future-state routing is expected to be tied to threshold plus department or cost center.
After PO approval in DocuShare, P2P receives an email showing approval and Juliette adds the item to her Excel tracker.
If an invoice exceeds the PO, Juliette reaches out manually for explanation and applies some leeway. No configured percent or amount tolerance rule exists in-system today.
Once the invoice is checked against the PO in the spreadsheet, AP books it in Sage and creates the vendor there if needed.
Only Juliette and Fatma can create vendors in Sage today.
Juliette enters bank information into Sage; Trustpair validates banking information twice per day.
No formal aging report exists and there is effectively no usable reporting layer across France P2P today.
Cost coding is broken: entire multi-component projects may be booked to single item#. Pass-throughs difficult to track.
No formal vendor onboarding/selection process — Trustpair validates banking at wire only, not at vendor creation.
Trinidad P2P bundle received: 10 files including approval matrices, SOPs, payment workbook, DocuShare guide, supplier master cleaning workbook.
CFTI P2P RCM received and structure confirmed. 5 ERP-signal controls confirmed (FR-PTP-02G, 04G, 05G, 06G, 14G).
Strategic framing confirmed by Trinidad (Mar 2 call): SOX gaps drive Phase 1 ERP design. Automation/efficiency = Phase 2+ post Jan 1, 2027.

4-step France P2P process formally documented in SOP0084_V01 (effective 09-SEP-2024, source: ABIVAX_Purchase process ENG): Step 1 Receipt — invoice received via DocuShare/email inbox monitored by Juliette/Fatma/Philippe. Step 2 Entry — AP codes and enters into Sage ACHA journal with analytical allocation (ADM/RAD 20/80 split) and project codes. Step 3 Validation — DocuShare approval routing per DoA threshold matrix; 48-hour cutoff before payment run. Step 4 Payment — Trustpair validation gate (mandatory hard stop) then Agicap-connected bank transfer (Wells Fargo / SocGen), campaigns on 10th and 25th of each month.

SOP0084_V01 (Purchase Order and Contract Process) formally signed: authored by Hema Keshava (SVP Finance), quality reviewed by Ana Sharma, approved by Didier Blondel (CFO) and Marc de Garidel (CEO). Effective 09-SEP-2024. Policy confirmed: every PO must be connected to a signed contract — no standalone POs.
SVP Finance pre-approval required for all Abivax LLC contracts (US entity). This is a policy constraint that must be reflected in NetSuite approval routing for US LLC transactions.

Trustpair is a mandatory hard gate per formal SOP: 'No invoice can be paid without prior validation by Trustpair.' The 48-hour cutoff before each payment campaign means Trustpair validation and DocuShare approval must be completed by the 8th and 23rd of each month.

Delegation of Authority matrix confirmed (source: Approval PO matrix.pdf + Approval Invoices Authorization Matrix.pdf). PO/Contract approval thresholds: ≤€50K — SVP, VPs, or Sr Directors; €50K–€500K — Chiefs (C-suite -1); €500K–€750K — SVP Finance (Hema Keshava); €750K–€1M — CFO (Didier Blondel); >€1M — CEO (Marc de Garidel).

Invoice approval matrix — WITHOUT purchase order: ≤€50K — Project Manager + SVP/VP; >€50K–€500K — Chiefs; >€500K–€750K — SVP Finance; >€750K–€1M — CFO; >€1M — CEO. This is the higher-risk track (no committed spend baseline).

Invoice approval matrix — WITH purchase order: ≤€50K — Project Manager only; ≤€200K — PM + SVP/VP; >€200K–€750K — Chiefs; >€750K–€2M — SVP Finance + CFO (dual approval); >€2M — CEO. Lower thresholds than without-PO track reflect PO pre-commitment as a control.

Contract signature authority is a separate policy layer above operational approval (source: Signature policy.pdf): Didier Blondel signs contracts ≤€500K; Marc de Garidel signs contracts >€500K. This is distinct from invoice and PO approval routing.

Analytical allocation confirmed: all invoices coded with (1) ADM vs. RAD designation at 20%/80% split for mixed costs, (2) project code, (3) cost center. This is the current-state cost coding structure that NetSuite COA design must accommodate.

DocuShare confirmed as formally controlled document routing tool in SOP (not tribal knowledge). Current role: receives invoice documents, routes for approval, issues email approval confirmation to AP team. Juliette logs the DocuShare approval email in her master Excel tracker before Sage booking.

Agicap is the live TMS for France SA payments; Sage 100 generates payment files and exports them automatically via SFTP to Agicap (C:\Agicap\Exports on Sage remote desktop), removed after export to avoid local retention.
EBICS TS contract governs the SocGen bank connection via Agicap; €10M per-file limit at SocGen; €5M threshold in Agicap (liftable by admin or Roxandra for payments €5–10M).
Agicap does not have a SOC 2 report today; report is on Agicap's 2026 roadmap.
Agicap SoD gap: no co-administration requirement; a single administrator can create or modify a vendor or profile without a second approver.
Agicap cannot distinguish USD treasury transfers from supplier third-party transfers, creating a validation-rights risk if the Treasurer can approve USD payments.
CFGI conclusion (SOX): Abivax cannot rely on Agicap alone as the payment signing tool from a SOX perspective; must separately validate that payments on the bank statement were approved and signed.
currentStateKnown.source: RE Abivax x CFGI - Retour sur Agicap SOX

currentStateKnown.fact: Payment cancellation vulnerability: a payment cancelled in Agicap 'A signer' returns to 'A preparer', at which point the bank account of an existing third party can be changed and the payment resent for signature without triggering an alert email.
Only two payment batches per month are run, making a bank-statement-to-approval reconciliation feasible as a formalized compensating control (per CFGI).
currentStateKnown.source: RE Abivax x CFGI - Retour sur Agicap SOX

currentStateKnown.fact: Nightly audit trail CSV is generated at C:\Script\Export_Events (hidden, read-only directory); only Juliette and Fatma have write access to the Agicap export folder.

currentStateUnknown.fact: US LLC P2P current-state flow and evidence practices — Matt Epley confirmed docs in progress as of Feb 25; still not received by Mar 2.
Trustpair integration model for NetSuite: standard bundled app vs. custom build vs. paid app — not yet confirmed with vendor.
Tolerance policy for invoice-over-PO exceptions is not formalized: percent threshold, absolute amount threshold, approver path, and hard-stop behavior still need design.
Where control evidence is retained today for completed wire transfers (Trustpair log? Sage? Email?).
Contractor volume and spend — no count or $ figure available; not tracked.

erpDesignRequirements.fact: PRE-ERP (governance, not NetSuite): Delegation of Authority matrix — who approves what at what spend threshold. Must be built before blueprint; ERP enforces it, it doesn't create it.
PRE-ERP (policy decision): PO requirement for contractors — yes/no decision needed before configuration. Change management required if yes.
System-enforced PO approval workflow with configurable approval tiers by spend threshold and entity.
Approval routing should support threshold plus department or cost center, not amount alone.
Trustpair integration at vendor onboarding (not just at wire) — integration model to be confirmed with NetSuite (standard app / custom / paid).
Two-way and three-way match capability for invoice processing.
Invoice matching needs configurable percent and or amount tolerance thresholds for over-PO invoices, with explicit exception routing.
Vendor setup workflow with named approver and Trustpair validation gate before vendor is live.
Role-based access / SOD enforcement on vendor setup and payment authorization.
Payment run traceable from PO to payment with system-retained evidence (not email-based).
Commitment register: all PO-backed commitments tracked in NetSuite by go-live — closes $200M+ untracked commitment gap.
France P2P reporting baseline must include AP aging, open PO aging, exception reporting, and payment status reporting inside the ERP.
Entity-level separation: France SA vs US LLC must be independently operable under common framework.
PHASE 2+ (post Jan 1, 2027): CRO pass-through tracking, contractor PO workflows, cost coding optimization — not Phase 1 scope.

pillars.label: Record to Report
pillars.currentStateSummary: Financial close is a manual, Excel-heavy process executed by KPMG (Robin Lapous) on a quarterly basis with no system support for consolidation or IFRS adjustments. Sage 100 lacks the data dimensions (country tag, cost center structure) needed for multi-entity reporting. No Chart of Accounts designed for NetSuite. GL/CoA design is the most consequential pre-blueprint decision — it affects every downstream report, rollup, and audit test. Treasury (cash, bank connectivity) is a sub-workstream owned by Roxandra Sturdza (FR) and Kimberly Gordon (US).
pillars.futureStateSummary: NetSuite as the system of record for France SA and US LLC. Quarterly close executed in-system with audit-ready evidence retention. GL/CoA designed for multi-entity, multi-GAAP operation. Treasury integrated for cash visibility and bank connectivity. Manual Excel bridge eliminated or scoped down to exceptional items only.
pillars.whyItMatters: This is the core of what the ERP replaces: a fragmented, KPMG-dependent, manually-bridged financial close. Getting R2R right in NetSuite is the primary deliverable that justifies the program investment and enables the US public launch reporting infrastructure.

complianceLayer.note: The 84 Controls/Audit CFTI controls span ITGC, SOD, SDLC, and financial close controls. The ERP-signal controls within this workstream must be built into NetSuite configuration — not compensating controls added post go-live.

pillars.label: Reporting & Planning
pillars.currentStateSummary: French GAAP to IFRS/SEC bridge is quarterly-paced, manually executed in Excel, and heavily dependent on KPMG (Robin Lapous). No formal process documentation exists. Workday Adaptive/Interactive is in place for FP&A but underused. Current Sage data model lacks a country tag and other dimensions needed for multi-entity reporting. US SEC reporting managed by internal Finance but bridged manually.
pillars.futureStateSummary: NetSuite should deliver a stronger quarterly reporting operating model immediately — better data dimensions, cleaner Sage-to-reporting extract path, and reduced manual bridge dependency where practical. Monthly reporting and commercial/royalty complexity are later-phase scope (2027/2028), not core ERP v1.
pillars.whyItMatters: Without a reliable reporting data model in NetSuite, Abivax cannot scale toward US public launch reporting requirements. The ERP is the foundation that makes quarterly → monthly → SEC-grade reporting possible without manual bridging and KPMG dependency for every close.

pillars.summary: Juliette France P2P fact capture added. Updated P2P current-state to reflect DocuShare approval emails feeding Juliette's master Excel tracker, amount-only approval routing, manual invoice-over-PO exception handling with leeway, vendor creation restricted to Juliette/Fatma in Sage, Juliette bank entry with Trustpair twice-daily validation, and the absence of formal aging or usable P2P reporting. Added ERP requirements for cost-center-based approval routing, invoice tolerance thresholds, spreadsheet replacement, and France P2P reporting.

changeLog.summary: Initial synthesis created from erp_pillar_baselines.json, cfti_control_register.json, erp_pillars.json, process_flow_diagram_payloads.json, timeline.json, and presentations.json. All four pillars populated. Program governance section added. Confidence levels set to 'partial' across P2P, Reporting, and Controls — all three are in active fact-finding phase. Governance set to 'strong' given selection decision is confirmed.

Email analysis pass on 166 emails (Feb 16–Mar 2 window). Key updates: (1) P2P — Trinidad's 10-file France P2P bundle confirmed received; US P2P docs still pending from Matt Epley (expected Feb 25-26, not yet arrived). (2) Reporting — Frederick Golly FP&A request has no reply by Mar 2; Robin Lapous confirmed manual Excel, no docs. (3) Controls — CFGI engaged, FSCP + P2P RCM delivered; ITGC pending; Adrian Holbrook clarified as Sr. Financial Analyst (consolidator only) — escalate controls content to Youness Tyamaz. (4) Governance — NetSuite implementation timeline XLSX found in Jamal Azil's Feb 23 email (may close Audit deck Gap #2); Camille OOO March 6 flagged as scheduling risk; contract law options circulated (3 structures, legal review started); Board deck due Mar 13 to Didier. Program-level risks updated.

P2P current-state call with Trinidad Mesa + Philippe Goncalves. Major P2P pillar update: volumes now confirmed (4,000 invoices/yr, 650 POs, $200M+ commitments, <500 vendors). System landscape locked: Sage/DocuShare/Trustpair/Concur with zero feeds — all manual. Key gaps surfaced: no DoA (pre-ERP governance deliverable), no PO for contractors, CRO contracts without POs, $200M+ commitments untracked, cost coding broken. Philippe to send Sage XLS + Trustpair report. Strategic framing from Trinidad: SOX-first design in Phase 1; automation/efficiency is Phase 2+ post Jan 1, 2027. currentStateSummary, currentStateKnown, erpDesignRequirements, nextMoves, waitingOn all updated. Meeting notes at collab/claude/outputs/p2p-meeting-notes-2026-03-02.md.

Philippe Goncalves workbooks analyzed (received Mar 2). (1) P2P: vendor master confirmed 497 vendors; Trustpair validation results mapped — 475 Favorable, 22 couple-Défavorable (pre-migration SOX gate), 10 Défavorable/Anomalie. FR-PTP-02G evidence confirmed. ENGAGE: €165M EUR active commitments, 2,888 lines, 187 vendors. confidenceLevel upgraded to 'established'. 3 migration-readiness nextMoves added. (2) Governance: KPMG commercials confirmed (€650K Year 1, €1.02M 3-year, 392 man-days). $1M Year 1 assumption retired. currentStateKnown updated in program-governance pillar and topProgramRisks.

Pillar restructure v1→v2. Consolidated from 4 pillars to 3 functional workstream pillars: P2P (unchanged), Record to Report (new — absorbs GL/CoA, close, treasury from old Controls/Governance context), Reporting & Planning (renamed/refocused from Reporting/Data). Controls/Audit and Program Governance demoted to _archivedPillars. SOX controls now a complianceLayer field within each pillar. Governance/mobilization data stays in programState. Rationale: the old 4-pillar structure over-weighted SOX compliance as a program objective; the 3-pillar structure reflects the actual ERP workstreams the program is delivering.

Program budget EUR 999,936 approved — Didier Blondel verbal authorization March 2026.

_archivedPillars.label: Controls / Auditability
_archivedPillars.currentStateSummary: 143 CFTI controls reviewed across P2P, FSCP, and IT. 45 out-of-scope for ERP. 9 ERP-signal controls identified. Active remediation tracker exists (CFTI macro/micro views with control IDs and statuses). ITGC RCM is pending final validation. Documented SOX findings and deficiency inventory not yet loaded into program. Evidence retention is fragmented across systems.
_archivedPillars.futureStateSummary: ERP should be governed as a control-remediation program, not just a technology rollout. NetSuite must embed approvals, SOD, and audit evidence retention into system design — not as compensating controls added after go-live. 9 ERP-signal controls are the minimum design checkpoints; all must have named remediation owners before blueprint starts.
_archivedPillars.whyItMatters: Abivax is a US-listed biotech with SOX compliance obligations. The Audit Committee is an active oversight body. If the ERP goes live without addressing documented control deficiencies, the company carries forward known audit risk into a higher-scrutiny environment. This is the pillar where the most reputational and governance risk lives.

cftiControls.note: 9 ERP-signal controls span P2P and FSCP. ITGC controls pending final validation from CFTI.

currentStateKnown: CFTI FSCP RCM and P2P RCM both received and structure confirmed (control IDs, statuses, remediation columns).
Remediation tracker exists in CFTI macro/micro views — active tracking of gap categories and owners.
IT Master Plan (Jade) confirms SOX compliance is an explicit IT workstream for 2026-2027.
IT org: Didier → Christophe Hennequin → Jade Nguyen / Benjamin / Erwan.
At least one vendor-management gap is tied to a Sage material weakness finding.
9 ERP-addressable control gaps identified across P2P and FSCP — these are the minimum ERP blueprint checkpoints.
currentStateUnknown: Documented SOX findings / deficiency inventory not yet loaded — Hema response still pending.
Finance vs IT remediation ownership split not confirmed in one place.
ITGC RCM not yet finalized — expected from CFTI after final validation (was 'next week' as of Feb 25).
Evidence retention paths for critical flows not mapped.
Explicit prioritization of 9 ERP-signal controls for blueprint sequencing not yet done.

Controls slides for Audit Committee: Adrian Holbrook (Sr. Financial Analyst, FP&A) asked Mike to provide the slides. Mike is the author; Adrian will present. Mike should source controls content from CFGI (Youness Tyamaz) to populate the slides.
erpDesignRequirements: All 9 ERP-signal controls must be assigned a named owner with a dated remediation commitment before ERP blueprint begins.
Control-by-design: approval workflows, SOD, and audit evidence retention must be designed into ERP configuration — not managed outside the system.
Each data feed and system integration must have a named owner, monitoring step, and failure escalation path.
US and France process variants must be rationalized within a common control framework — divergence must be bounded and documented.
Evidence retention path must be system-retained by transaction type, not manually assembled per audit cycle.

keyDecisionsPending.decision: Finance vs IT ownership split on remediation tracking
keyDecisionsPending.impact: Without clear ownership, remediation stalls at the same point it's been stuck. Must be resolved before April mobilization.

keyDecisionsPending.decision: Which of the 9 ERP-signal controls are sequenced into Phase 1 blueprint vs later phases
keyDecisionsPending.impact: Determines whether the ERP addresses the audit committee's core concerns at go-live or defers them.

keyDecisionsPending.decision: Evidence repository design: where does control evidence live post-ERP (NetSuite native vs separate tool)
keyDecisionsPending.impact: Audit evidence repository is a pre-condition for audit readiness — must be designed, not assumed.

nextMoves: Follow up on ITGC RCM from CFTI (was expected 'next week' as of Feb 25).
Create controls slides for Adrian Holbrook to present at Audit Committee — Mike is author; source narrative from CFGI (Youness Tyamaz) RCM documents.
Extract deficiency inventory from Hema/CFTI responses and classify: ERP-addressable / non-ERP remediation / management decision.
Map 9 ERP-signal controls to named owners and draft dated remediation commitments.
Use CFTI tracker structure as baseline format for audit committee reporting.
waitingOn: Hema: SOX findings, remediation tracker location, control narratives
Jade: IT control gaps, interface monitoring docs, admin ownership matrix (English version in progress with Denis)

_archivedPillars._archived: 2026-03-04: Demoted from top-level pillar. Controls/SOX is a compliance design layer within P2P and R2R workstreams, not a standalone deliverable. Key data preserved here for reference. CFTI register at data/abivax/cfti_control_register.json.

_archivedPillars.label: Program Governance & Mobilization
_archivedPillars.currentStateSummary: NetSuite selected Feb 26, 2026 following a structured Sep 2025–Feb 2026 selection process with KPMG support and vendor demos. Program is now in the post-selection window. No contract signed, no implementation scope confirmed, no implementation team assembled. April 2026 mobilization is the target.
_archivedPillars.futureStateSummary: By April 2026: commercial terms agreed with NetSuite, implementation scope confirmed, KPMG advisory role defined, internal capacity committed by function, governance cadence established. From April through Jan 1, 2027: blueprint → build → test → go-live with weekly governance and monthly exec/board updates.
_archivedPillars.whyItMatters: The implementation mobilization window is 6–8 weeks. Every week lost between now and April creates timeline compression in the 9-month implementation window. The go-live date is non-negotiable — scope decisions and resourcing commitments are the only variables.
currentStateKnown: NetSuite commercials confirmed via Camille Girard KPMG deck (Feb 26 2026 Audit Committee meeting): Build (one-off) €463,120 | Run (annual SaaS) €187,009/year | Year 1 total ≈ €650,128 | 3-year total €1,024,145.80. Implementation: 392 man-days at €1,135.51/day. Replaces $1M Year 1 budget assumption used in prior planning. (_archived)
NetSuite selected. Competing vendor was SAP — SAP eliminated due to proposal/RACI concerns and governance/PMO sufficiency questions.

KPMG played advisory role in selection (Camille Girard / Aymen). Implementation advisory role confirmed: none. KPMG role limited to statutory audit only (Camille Girard, Audit Committee liaison). Decided 2026-03-18. (_archived)
IT org confirmed: Didier → Christophe Hennequin → Jade/Benjamin/Erwan. IT roadmap explicitly includes ERP in 2026-2027 framing.
Implementation vendor: NetSuite directly or NetSuite partner — not yet determined.
Governance model target: weekly program governance, monthly exec update, board-cycle readout, milestone-triggered escalation.
NetSuite implementation timeline XLSX exists: Jamal Azil sent 'ABIVAX - Draft-Implementation Timeline V2.xlsx' on Feb 23 (email attachment). Retrieve before declaring Audit deck Gap #2 still open.
Contract law options shared by Camille (Feb 27): three structures under consideration (French law / US law / split). Legal review initiated with Thomas Descusses, Olivia Bui, Peggy Gambardella.
Board ERP checkpoint confirmed Mar 19 — Didier requested deck draft by Mar 13. Didier unavailable Feb 26-Mar 16 (US bank conferences).
Camille Girard OOO on March 6 — if Audit Committee meeting is that date, confirm logistics with Hema.
currentStateUnknown: Commercial negotiation owner: Mike Markman + Hema Keshava (confirmed Mar 2). Budget assumption: $1M Year 1, scale naturally thereafter. Contract timeline still to be locked with NetSuite.
Implementation scope: not confirmed. No contract, no blueprint start date.
KPMG advisory engagement: ERP selection KPMG contacts (Camille/Aymen) vs separate implementation advisory team — roles not separated. (_archived)
Internal capacity commitments by function — Finance, IT, FP&A availability for ERP workstreams unknown.
Executive decision rights model — who can approve scope changes, escalate blockers, authorize budget.

keyDecisionsPending.decision: Commercial negotiation: who owns the NetSuite contract negotiation and by when
keyDecisionsPending.impact: Blocks implementation start. Every week without a signed contract is a week lost from the 9-month window.

keyDecisionsPending.decision: Implementation scope confirmation: which modules, which entities, which integrations in Phase 1
keyDecisionsPending.impact: Without confirmed scope, resourcing plan, KPMG advisory scope, and internal commitments are all floating. (_archived)

keyDecisionsPending.decision: KPMG implementation advisory engagement: scope and owner (_archived)
keyDecisionsPending.resolution: No KPMG implementation advisory engagement. CFGI selected as SI lead. KPMG = statutory audit only. (_archived)
keyDecisionsPending.impact: KPMG knows the Abivax control environment. Whether they play an advisory role in implementation affects both quality and timeline. (_archived)
Without defined decision rights, governance cadence is performative. Escalation paths must be clear before the first implementation meeting.

nextMoves: Draft post-selection notification email to Camille (cc Hema): confirm owners, negotiation path, and timing toward April work start.
Confirm internal capacity commitments by function before April — Finance, IT, FP&A.
Separate KPMG selection-phase contacts from implementation-phase advisory contacts. (_archived)
Establish governance cadence: weekly program, monthly exec, board-cycle readout.
Present governance model at Board (Mar 19) and Audit Committee (Mar 6) meetings.
waitingOn: Hema: internal capacity commitments; KPMG advisory scope decision closed — no KPMG implementation advisory (CFGI selected as SI lead). KPMG limited to statutory audit only. Decided 2026-03-18 (_archived)
Board / Audit Committee: endorse governance model and control-by-design principle
